heading: Office of Justice Programs grants, cooperative agreements, and contracts
label: 10110 Office of Justice Programs grants, cooperative agreements, and contracts

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Department of Justice Appropriations Act, 1999, and also as part of the Omnibus Consolidated and Emergency Supplemental Appropriations Act, 1999, and not as part of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 which comprises this chapter.Section was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 3715 of Title 42Section was also formerly classified as a note under prior to transfer to .section 3712 of Title 42section 3715 of Title 42

Amendments2002— substituted “any fiscal year the Attorney General—” for “fiscal year 1999, the Assistant Attorney General for the Office of Justice Programs of the Department of Justice—” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 107–2732001—Par. (1). , inserted “(including, notwithstanding any contrary provision of law (unless the same should expressly refer to this section), any organization that administers any program established in title 1 of )” after “that Office”.Pub. L. 107–56, § 614(1)Public Law 90–351Par. (2). , inserted “functions, including any” after “all” and “(including, notwithstanding any contrary provision of law (unless the same should expressly refer to this section), any organization that administers any program established in title 1 of )” after “that Office”.Pub. L. 107–56, § 614Public Law 90–351

Use of Grants for Post-Conviction Relief Representation, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–73, § 4139 Stat. 2008Jan. 23, 2026“The Office of Justice Programs or the Office on Violence Against Women, in awarding a grant that may be used for legal representation, may not prohibit a recipient from using the grant for legal representation for post-conviction relief.”

Office of Justice Programs Grants, Cooperative Agreements, and Contracts, , , 1501A–20, as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–113, div. B, § 1000(a)(1) [title I, § 108(a)]113 Stat. 1535Pub. L. 107–56, title VI, § 614115 Stat. 370Nov. 29, 1999Oct. 26, 2001“Notwithstanding any other provision of law, for fiscal year 2000, the Assistant Attorney General for the Office of Justice Programs of the Department of Justice—“(1)may make grants, or enter into cooperative agreements and contracts, for the Office of Justice Programs and the component organizations of that Office (including, notwithstanding any contrary provision of law (unless the same should expressly refer to this section), any organization that administers any program established in title 1 of [see References in Text note above]); andPublic Law 90–351“(2)shall have final authority over all functions, including any grants, cooperative agreements and contracts made, or entered into, for the Office of Justice Programs and the component organizations of that Office (including, notwithstanding any contrary provision of law (unless the same should expressly refer to this section), any organization that administers any program established in title 1 of [see References in Text note above]), except for grants made under the provisions of sections 201, 202, 301, and 302 of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 [, 10122, 10131, 10132], as amended; and sections 204(b)(3), 241(e)(1), 243(a)(1), 243(a)(14) and 287A(3) of the Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention Act of 1974 [ and former , 5653(a)(1), (14), 5667d–1(3)], as amended.”Public Law 90–35134 U.S.C. 1012134 U.S.C. 11114(b)(3)42 U.S.C. 5651(e)(1)[, , , 2762A–67, provided that: ]Pub. L. 106–553, § 1(a)(2) [title I, § 108]114 Stat. 2762Dec. 21, 2000“Section 108(a) of the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2000 (as enacted into law by ) [set out above] shall apply for fiscal year 2001 and thereafter.”section 1000(a)(1) of Public Law 106–113
